author: Riaz-ud-din
title: Guldasta-i-Intakhab-i-Sukhan
notes: Selected-ghazal anthology, closing the URDU—POETRY section.

author: Abdul Hayi, Maulvi
title: Al-Mudafi-ul-Ilahiyya
gloss: A booklet written in refutation of the tenets of the Babi and the Bahai religious sects of modern Persia.
notes: Opens the large URDU—RELIGION section with a Sunni refutation of Babism/Baha'ism — the inter-sectarian polemic (munazara) genre; author based in Rangoon (a diaspora-Muslim registrant), showing the Burma-Punjab print circuit. The refutation-of-heresy mode dominates this section.

author: Abdulla Imadi
title: Gham-i-Hussain
gloss: A pamphlet condemning the ceremonials in the month of Muharram.
notes: A reformist (Deobandi/Ahl-i-Hadith-style) condemnation of Muharram mourning ceremonies, 2000 copies — the intra-Sunni critique of Shia/popular ritual; the purификation-of-practice reform current attacking the very Karbala-mourning that other entries (Rauzat p23, the Ali-lives) serve. Sectarian tension within Islam on display.

author: Abdur Rahman
title: Zarurat-i-Zamana
gloss: A book in the form of questions and answers in which the writer has tried to prove that the principles of Islam are true and reasonable.
notes: 'The Need of the Age' — a rationalist apologia for Islam in catechetical form (proving Islam 'reasonable'), the modernist-apologetic response to Christian-missionary and rationalist challenge; the 'Islam-as-rational-religion' argument (cf. Sayyid Ahmad Khan's legacy). From Kapurthala state.

title: Defence Islam, Hissa Awwal
gloss: A pamphlet in which the author has tried to show that the belief of Christians and Mirzais regarding the crucifixion of Christ is wrong, and that the Islamic belief on the point is correct.
notes: Anti-crucifixion polemic targeting BOTH Christians AND 'Mirzais' (Ahmadis/Qadianis) — the three-way Sunni vs Christian vs Ahmadi controversy over the death of Christ (the Ahmadiyya's distinctive doctrine that Jesus survived the cross). The Qadian/Ahmadi controversy is a live Punjab-specific theological battleground; author styles himself 'Bible Reformer' from Jammu. Notable for the anti-Ahmadi front.

author: Drashna Nand
title: Ulama-i-Islam se Sawalat
gloss: Questions for the Muhammadan theologians, containing certain objections against Islam.
notes: 'Questions for the Ulama of Islam' — an Arya-Samaj-style challenge/objection tract against Islam by a Hindu author (Darshana Nand, cf. his Arya-Samaj polemic p32 s7); the Arya-Samaj vs Islam munazara warfare that inflamed Punjab communal relations. The shuddhi/critique-of-Islam front. Directly on-theme for communal-polemic dynamics.

author: Fayyaz Hussain, M.
title: Kalam-ul-Matin
gloss: A controversial book in which the writer, who is a Shia, tries to prove that Ali, the Fourth Caliph and the son-in-law of Muhammad, was the rightful heir to the caliphate after the prophet, and that the first three caliphs had no right whatever.
publisher: Sayad Saghir Hassan, Proprietor, Yusufi Press
notes: An explicitly Shia polemic asserting Ali's rightful succession and denying the first three caliphs' legitimacy, 232 pp. — the Shia-Sunni imamate controversy at its sharpest; the catalogue's own note flags it 'controversial'. Directly documents intra-Muslim sectarian conflict (the Yusufi Press of Delhi, a Shia-leaning imprint given its Ali-focused output this quarter).
